title: Decision Log Entry
description: Validates each entry in master.decision_log[]. Append-only per § 6.3 — skills append, never mutate prior entries. Full decision_type enum per § 6.2.

id.description: Stable per-entry identifier (conventionally monotonic dl-NNN). NOT an ordering key: the server orders decision_log by an internal dl_seq column, so a caller-supplied id decouples from true order. dl_seq (server-internal) is authoritative for ordering; id is a stable identifier only per § 6.3.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the decision. Informational only — the server-internal dl_seq column is the authoritative ordering, not id or timestamp.

skill.description: The skill that appended this entry. Skills only append, never write to others' sections.

cycle_slug.description: Cycle this decision belongs to. null for org-level decisions (e.g., master_init, org_metadata_updated).

decision_type.description: Full enum per § 6.2. 25 values covering cycle lifecycle, org lifecycle, all 4 skill scopes, cross-skill shared events, and the 5 comp-suite v2 lifecycle events (P2): mode-default, pre-engagement null-out, engagement close, cycle-gating bypass, too-early pre-engagement mode choice. Mirrors the server VALID_DECISION_TYPES frozenset + the 0064 CHECK exactly.

items.description: Free-form lowercase tags. Hyphens and underscores allowed.

summary.description: Operator-readable summary of the decision and rationale. Free-text, multi-line allowed. Soft warning (app layer) if capitalized two-word pattern matching person name detected per § 14.2.

refs.description: Optional pointers to related artifacts per § 6.1.

cycle_artifact.description: Path to the primary cycle artifact this decision references.

council_file.description: Path to the council deliberation file. Used with decision_type: council_outcome per § 6.5.

anti_pattern_id.description: ID of the anti-pattern promoted (e.g., 'ap-007'). Used with decision_type: anti_pattern_promoted.

related_decision_ids.description: Linked decision log entry IDs. Used for cycle_reopened audit chains per § 9.3.

examples.summary: Chose scenario B (modest 2.5% across-the-board) over scenario A (variable 1-4% by performance band) for pharmacy-qc-fy26. Rationale: union sensitivity around perceived favoritism.
CHRO requested removal of 'merit' from all-hands-announcement; replaced with 'performance recognition'. Promoted to org-level anti-pattern.
